campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and a detailed breakdown of spending. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, such as better access to buses, biking, and walking paths.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
Monthly spending report and treasurer's accounting
New multimodal transportation plan to improve buses, biking, and pedestrian access

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could voice concerns about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public comments, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipped the usual first-round formal reading to speed up the process. The ordinance will be voted on at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for approval
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, primarily to approve official records from several previous meetings in March. The council also authorized the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are unclear from the provided agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of meeting minutes from March 16, 17, and 30
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Review of closed session and special study session discussions

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize upcoming agendas for April 21st and April 28th, 2026. The council reviewed what items should be added to these meeting agendas, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked on any legal notice requirements. The city clerk also presented public records from early April.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 21st council meeting agenda
Reviewed draft agenda for April 28th meeting
Set time slots for specific agenda items
Checked legal notice requirements for agenda items and documents
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The city council held a study session to review Mountain View's spending plans for building and improving city infrastructure in fiscal year 2026-27. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week during the regular session.
keyTopics: 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program budget and planning
Teen Month Proclamation
National Volunteer Week Proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review notes from their previous March meeting and introduced a new rule change about tenant protections for renters in Sunnyvale. The meeting focused on housing issues and how the city protects people living in rental properties.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25 meeting minutes
New tenant protection rules being proposed
Changes to Chapter 19.71 of city housing codes
Residential renter safeguards

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026 to teach residents how to use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends taxpayer money. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city—those trying to influence decisions—to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo for residents
City budget overview and spending visibility
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under municipal code
How to track legislative and administrative influence attempts

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 15, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 15, 2026 to discuss updates to the city's Charter (its basic governing rules). Chair Pat Nikolai participated remotely from Oregon. The city is continuing to allow residents to join meetings either in person or online via Zoom.
keyTopics: Charter Review Committee meetings happening in-person
Remote participation available via Zoom for residents
Written public comments accepted via email to cityattorney@santaclaraca.gov
Chair Pat Nikolai attending from out-of-state location

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative matters, including updates to federal housing and community development plans for 2025-2026, review of new project requests from individual council members, and a proposed change to how the city fills council vacancies. The meeting also included routine approvals of previous meeting minutes and a preview of upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Housing and community development plan updates (2025-2030)
New project requests from council members
Changes to how council vacancies are filled
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner is asking the city to officially recognize the building as more historically important than it currently is, and in return would get permission to add 5,000 square feet of new space for renovation and earthquake safety improvements.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic status upgrade request
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for renovation and seismic safety
Downtown commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule approval
March 12, 2026 meeting minutes confirmed
